en.title: The invisible grammar of white space
en.excerpt: White space is not the absence of design — it is design. Every margin carries intentional weight.
en.body:
## Introduction

White space is not the absence of design — it is design. Every margin, every gap, every pause between elements carries intentional weight.

Designers new to the craft often treat negative space as leftover room — territory that simply hasn't been filled yet.

## Why space communicates hierarchy

In any composition, the amount of breathing room around an element signals its importance.

> "Space is the breath of design. Take it away and the work suffocates."

The principle holds at the micro scale too. Letter-spacing, line-height, the padding inside a button — each shapes how confident and readable a piece of design feels.

## Space as emotional tone

Beyond hierarchy, white space is one of the most powerful tools for establishing emotional register. Luxury brands use it to signal exclusivity — the wider the margins, the more precious the contents feel.

zh.title: 留白的隐形语法
zh.excerpt: 留白不是设计的缺失——它本身就是设计。每一个边距都承载着有意的重量。
zh.body:
## 引言

留白不是设计的缺失——它本身就是设计。每一个边距、每一个间隙、每一个元素之间的停顿都承载着有意的重量。

初学设计的人往往把负空间视为剩余空间——那些尚未被填满的区域。

## 为什么空间传达层次感

在任何构图中，元素周围的留白量都在传递其重要性。

> "空间是设计的呼吸。把它拿走，作品就会窒息。"

这一原则在微观层面同样适用。字间距、行高、按钮内的留白——这些无形的决定塑造了设计作品的可读性。

## 空间作为情感基调

除了层次感，留白还是建立情感基调最有力的工具之一。奢侈品牌用它来传达专属感——边距越宽，内容就显得越珍贵。

en.title: How I approach a design system audit
en.excerpt: Before you can build, you need to understand what already exists — and why it exists that way.
en.body:
## Starting with what's there

Before designing a single new component, I spend time with what's already there. Design system audits are unglamorous work, but they're the difference between building something that integrates and something that conflicts.

## The inventory phase

My process starts with inventory: every button, every type style, every spacing token currently in use — documented without judgment.

> "The goal isn't to delete variation, but to understand it."

Sometimes inconsistency is a sign of an evolving product. Sometimes it's technical debt. You need to know which before you start consolidating.

zh.title: 我如何进行设计系统审查
zh.excerpt: 在构建之前，你需要了解已有的内容——以及它为什么以这种方式存在。
zh.body:
## 从现有内容开始

在设计任何新组件之前，我会花时间了解已有的内容。设计系统审查是平凡的工作，但它决定了你构建的是融合一致还是相互冲突的东西。

## 清单阶段

我的流程从清单开始：每一个按钮、每一种字体样式、每一个当前使用的间距标记——不带评判地记录下来。

> "目标不是消除变体，而是理解它。"

有时不一致是产品演进的信号，有时是技术债务。在开始整合之前，你需要知道是哪种情况。

en.title: What typography taught me about listening
en.excerpt: Reading type carefully is practice for reading everything else carefully — including people.
en.body:
## Seeing type differently

When you train yourself to see type — really see it — something shifts in how you pay attention to the world.

The word "luxury" set in Helvetica feels different to the same word in a swash serif. The message is identical. The meaning is not.

## A transferable skill

This is close reading applied to visual form. And it's a transferable skill.

**Design is fundamentally about listening.** To what a brief actually asks for beneath what it says. To what a user does versus what they tell you.

zh.title: 排版教会我如何倾听
zh.excerpt: 仔细阅读字体排版是练习仔细阅读其他一切事物的方式——包括人。
zh.body:
## 以不同方式看待字体

当你训练自己去看字体——真正地看它——你对世界的关注方式就会发生转变。

用Helvetica排版的

与用华丽衬线字体排版的同一个词感觉不同。信息是相同的，意义却不同。

## 一种可迁移的技能

这是应用于视觉形式的精细阅读，是一种可迁移的技能。

**设计从根本上是关于倾听的。** 倾听任务简报在表面之下真正要求的内容，倾听用户的实际行为而非他们的描述。
